title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: data officer runs single command arriving dataset: prints one-line console summary, writes HTML report, returns list(status, report_path, snapshot_id) invisibly.
code: Your data file                  Your YAML config       │                                │       ▼                                ▼   read_dataset()            load_config()   (all columns as           (global defaults +    character, trimmed)       per-dataset rules)       │                                │       └──────────────┬─────────────────┘                      ▼           run_qc_checks()        ← single-snapshot checks (QC / SC series)           run_comparison_checks()← vs previous delivery   (CP series)           run_custom_checks()    ← your own rules                      │                      ▼           write_snapshot()       ← SQLite: one row per run           render_report()        ← self-contained HTML                      │                      ▼           run_dq_check()         ← the one function you call run_dq_check("customer_accounts", config_dir = "path/to/configs")

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: dqcheckr first-party package — install directly source: dependencies CRAN: readr, DBI, RSQLite, rmarkdown, knitr, kableExtra, ggplot2, gridExtra, yaml, dplyr.

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Two YAML files control every run.

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Placed config directory. Sets default thresholds used datasets unless dataset overrides .
code: snapshot_db:       "data/snapshots.sqlite" report_output_dir: "reports/"  default_rules:   # FAIL if any column's missing-value rate exceeds this (0–1)   max_missing_rate: 0.05    # FAIL if non-numeric values in a numeric column exceed this rate   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.01    # FAIL if row count drops below this (0 = disabled)   min_row_count: 0    # Version-comparison thresholds (used when a previous file is available)   max_row_count_change_pct:       0.10   max_numeric_mean_shift_pct:     0.20   max_missing_rate_change_pp:     2.0    # percentage points   max_non_numeric_rate_change_pp: 1.0    flag_new_columns:         true   flag_dropped_columns:     true   flag_type_changes:        true   flag_column_order_change: true   # WARN for CSV, FAIL for FWF

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: One file per dataset. keys specify needed — everything else inherits global defaults.
code: dataset_name: "customer_accounts"  # --- File location --- # Option A: folder scan (two most recently modified files are used) folder: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/"  # Option B: explicit paths (set current_file only → single-file mode, #           no version comparison) # current_file:  "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260410.csv" # previous_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260401.csv"  format:    csv encoding:  "UTF-8" delimiter: ","  # --- Schema contract (optional) --- # SC-01 flags extra columns; SC-02 flags missing columns. expected_columns:   - id   - name   - country_code   - account_status   - account_balance   - created_date  # --- Key columns (optional) --- # QC-12 checks each for duplicate values. key_columns:   - id  # --- Rule overrides (optional) --- # These replace the matching keys in default_rules for this dataset only. rule_overrides:   max_missing_rate:        0.02   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.05   min_row_count:           1000  # --- Per-column rules (optional) --- column_rules:   country_code:     allowed_values: ["GB", "US", "DE", "FR"]   # QC-09     pattern: "^[A-Z]{2}$"                       # QC-13   account_status:     allowed_values: ["ACTIVE", "CLOSED", "SUSPENDED"]   account_balance:     min_value: 0                                 # QC-10     max_value: 1000000  # --- Custom checks (optional) --- # custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"  # --- Output paths (optional, override global) --- # snapshot_db:       "data/snapshots.sqlite" # report_output_dir: "reports/"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: fields package requires run file location (folder current_file) format. Every config key optional — corresponding checks simply skipped key absent. minimal dataset config runs basic checks column-level rules:

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: fixed-width files, set format: fwf supply column widths names. file header row, set fwf_skip: 1 skip .
code: format: fwf fwf_skip: 1 fwf_widths:    [10, 20, 3, 10, 12, 11] fwf_col_names: [id, name, country_code, account_status, account_balance, created_date]

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: check returns one dq_result objects four key fields: check_id, status (PASS / WARN / FAIL / INFO), observed, message. overall run status worst single status across checks.

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: run current file alone.

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: run expected_columns set.

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: run previous file available. Skipped automatically single-file mode.

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: infer_col_type() classifies column whitespace trimming. Rules applied priority order: date — non-empty values parse known date format (%Y-%m-%d, %d/%m/%Y, %m/%d/%Y, %Y%m%d, %d-%m-%Y) numeric — ≥ 90% non-empty values coerce numeric character — everything else unknown — values empty NA 90% threshold means column 10% bad values still classified numeric, making bad values visible QC-11 (non-numeric contamination).
code: infer_col_type(c("2024-01-15", "2024-06-30"))  # "date" infer_col_type(c("100", "200", "N/A"))          # "character" (only 67% numeric) infer_col_type(c("100", "200", "N/A", rep("50", 17)))  # "numeric" (95% numeric) infer_col_type(c(NA, "", NA))                   # "unknown"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: HTML report contains: Header — dataset name, file name, run time, overall status badge File summary — row count, column count, file size, format, encoding Quality checks table — every check result, failures first Custom checks table — configured Version comparison — changed since previous delivery Historical trend — row count check outcomes across last 10 runs (appears least two runs recorded) Appendix — full column-level statistics table
code: result <- run_dq_check(   dataset_name = "customer_accounts",   config_dir   = "path/to/configs",   # contains dqcheckr.yml + customer_accounts.yml   open_report  = TRUE                 # open HTML in browser when interactive )  # Console output (always printed): # [dqcheckr] customer_accounts: FAIL — 1 warning(s), 2 failure(s). Report: reports/...html  result$status       # "PASS", "WARN", or "FAIL" result$report_path  # full path to the HTML file result$snapshot_id  # integer row ID in snapshots table

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: can call check function directly, without going full pipeline. Every check takes (df, config) returns list dq_result objects.
code: library(dqcheckr)  # Build a minimal config programmatically cfg <- list(   rules = list(     max_missing_rate     = 0.05,     max_non_numeric_rate = 0.01,     min_row_count        = 0   ),   column_rules     = list(     status = list(allowed_values = c("ACTIVE", "CLOSED"))   ),   key_columns      = "id",   expected_columns = NULL )  df <- read.csv("data/customer_accounts.csv", colClasses = "character") df[] <- lapply(df, trimws)  # Run a single check results <- check_missing_rate(df, cfg)  # Inspect results[[1]]$status    # "PASS" or "FAIL" results[[1]]$observed  # e.g. "3.2% missing (4 of 125)"  # Run all single-snapshot checks at once all_results <- run_qc_checks(df, cfg)  # Overall status overall_status(all_results)  # "FAIL" > "WARN" > "PASS" > "INFO"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Create plain .R file defines custom_checks(df). receives trimmed data frame must return list dq_result objects. Point dataset config: file sourced isolated environment (global session). dq_result() available inside . error — missing file, undefined function, runtime failure — stops run clear message.
code: # File: custom/customer_accounts_checks.R  custom_checks <- function(df) {   results <- list()    # Rule: ACTIVE accounts must not have a zero balance   active_zero <- df[df$account_status == "ACTIVE" &                     !is.na(df$account_balance) &                     df$account_balance == "0", ]   n <- nrow(active_zero)   results <- c(results, list(dq_result(     check_id   = "CUST-01",     check_name = "No zero-balance active accounts",     status     = if (n > 0) "FAIL" else "PASS",     observed   = sprintf("%d ACTIVE account(s) with balance 0", n),     message    = if (n > 0)       sprintf("%d ACTIVE account(s) have a zero balance.", n)     else       "No ACTIVE accounts have a zero balance."   )))    results } custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Every run writes two rows shared SQLite database: one row snapshots (run-level summary) one row per column-stat column_snapshots. datasets share one database, can query across : output/ directory (database file) created automatically first run exist.
code: library(DBI) library(RSQLite)  con <- dbConnect(SQLite(), "data/snapshots.sqlite")  # Recent runs for one dataset dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT id, file_name, overall_status, check_fail_count, run_timestamp    FROM snapshots    WHERE dataset_name = 'customer_accounts'    ORDER BY id DESC    LIMIT 10")  # Column-level stats for the most recent run dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT column_name, dq_check, value, threshold    FROM column_snapshots    WHERE snapshot_id = (      SELECT MAX(id) FROM snapshots WHERE dataset_name = 'customer_accounts'    )    ORDER BY column_name, dq_check")  dbDisconnect(con) dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT dataset_name, COUNT(*) AS runs,           SUM(check_fail_count) AS total_failures    FROM snapshots    GROUP BY dataset_name")

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: following uses dplyr::starwars exported CSV fixed-width files. Star Wars config sets max_missing_rate: 0.60 vehicles (87%) starships (77%) naturally sparse — characters none. columns FAIL, correct finding: genuinely incomplete consumer aware using . Expected console output:
code: # Config directory contains: #   dqcheckr.yml         — global thresholds #   starwars_csv.yml     — CSV dataset config #   starwars_fwf.yml     — FWF dataset config  # Run checks on both formats result_csv <- run_dq_check("starwars_csv", config_dir = "config", open_report = TRUE) result_fwf <- run_dq_check("starwars_fwf", config_dir = "config", open_report = TRUE) [dqcheckr] starwars_csv: FAIL — 0 warning(s), 2 failure(s). Report: output/reports/starwars_csv_....html [dqcheckr] starwars_fwf: FAIL — 0 warning(s), 2 failure(s). Report: output/reports/starwars_fwf_....html

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: package never modifies, moves, deletes source files.

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Owned evolvable. Every check plain R — readable, modifiable, hidden behind third-party API. Plain English outputs. Reports written data management staff, engineers. R code, package names, jargon. Fail loudly. Configuration errors, missing files, custom-check failures stop run clear message. Nothing silently skipped. Non-destructive. package reads files writes reports database records. Source files never touched.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: analytics organisation ingests external datasets multiple suppliers recurring basis. Verifying new delivery complete, structurally correct, consistent prior deliveries currently slow largely manual. Analysts spend time repetitive visual inspection instead higher-value work, quality problems risk missed caught late.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: dqcheckr purpose-built R package automates verification process. dataset arrival produces single, plain-English HTML report tells data management staff: Whether file structurally sound (columns, types, row count expected). Whether data values within acceptable bounds free obvious errors. delivery compares previous one — changed, drifted. delivery compares defined schema contract, one exists. Results also stored local SQLite database quality trends can tracked time across many deliveries.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Owned evolvable. tool first-party R code, thin wrapper around third-party API. Every check directly readable modifiable. Plain English outputs. Reports written data management staff, engineers. R code, package names, jargon. Fail loudly. Errors configuration, custom checks, parsing stop run clear message. Nothing silently skipped. Non-destructive. tool reads files writes reports database records. never modifies, moves, deletes source files.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: data officer runs single command arriving dataset: tool finds two recent files dataset’s folder, checks newer one, compares older one, writes timestamped HTML report reports folder, prints one-line summary console. report opens automatically browser. Files can also identified explicitly naming current previous file, rather relying folder modification times. Scale: tool designed 10 dataset arrivals per day across dataset types.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Data completeness column many missing values flagged. column entirely empty error. file fewer rows configured minimum, error. Data validity Columns expected contain specific values (e.g. country codes, status flags) checked permitted list. Unexpected values error. Numeric columns expected fall within range checked. --range values error. Columns expected match format (e.g. postcode pattern) checked regular expression. Violations error. Data integrity Columns designated keys (e.g. unique ID column) checked duplicates. Duplicates error. Rows entirely identical another row file flagged warning. Data consistency inferred data type column recorded. type change deliveries flagged. Non-numeric values column numeric flagged (warning present; error many). Schema contract (expected column list configured) Columns present file expected list error. Columns expected list absent file error. Custom checks (custom checks file configured dataset) Organisation-specific rules written R run file. Results appear alongside standard checks.

what: B.3 What is compared to the previous delivery
title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Row count: large change (increase decrease) flagged. Schema: new columns, dropped columns, type changes flagged. Missing rates: columns proportion missing values increased significantly flagged. Numeric distributions: columns average value shifted significantly flagged. Non-numeric contamination: columns proportion bad (non-numeric) values increased flagged. Distinct values: new values appeared values disappeared categorical columns listed. Column order: change order columns warning CSV files error fixed-width files (column order determines parsing).

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: HTML report — one file per run, self-contained (internet required view). Contains: header showing dataset name, file name, run time, overall status badge (green = clear, amber = warnings, red = failures). file summary (row count, column count, file size, format, encoding). quality checks table listing every check, status, observed, threshold, plain-English explanation. Failures warnings appear first. custom checks table (configured). version comparison section showing changed since previous delivery, plain-English narrative summary top. historical trend section showing row count check outcomes across last 10 runs (appears least two runs recorded). appendix full column-level statistics table. SQLite database — single database shared across datasets. Records summary statistics every run, enabling long-term trend queries without reading source files .

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
code: dqcheckr/ ├── R/ │   ├── run_check.R          # main entry point: run_dq_check() │   ├── ingest.R             # read CSV / FWF; trim whitespace │   ├── snapshot.R           # SQLite read/write │   ├── checks_generic.R     # QC-01..14 and SC-01..02 │   ├── checks_custom.R      # source and call custom_checks(df) │   ├── compare.R            # CP-01..08 │   ├── report.R             # rmarkdown::render() wrapper │   └── utils.R              # dq_result(), load_config(), infer_col_type(), helpers ├── inst/ │   ├── templates/report.Rmd │   └── config/ │       ├── dqcheckr.yml │       └── example_dataset.yml ├── tests/testthat/ │   ├── test-utils.R │   ├── test-checks.R │   ├── test-compare.R │   └── test-ingest.R ├── DESCRIPTION └── NAMESPACE

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Global config — dqcheckr.yml Per-dataset config — <dataset_name>.yml
code: snapshot_db: "data/snapshots.sqlite" report_output_dir: "reports/"  default_rules:   # Single-snapshot thresholds   max_missing_rate: 0.05            # FAIL if column missing rate exceeds this   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.01        # FAIL if non-numeric rate in numeric column exceeds this   min_row_count: 0                  # FAIL if row count below this (0 = disabled)    # Version comparison thresholds   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.10   max_numeric_mean_shift_pct: 0.20   max_missing_rate_change_pp: 2.0   # percentage points   max_non_numeric_rate_change_pp: 1.0    # Schema flags   flag_new_columns: true   flag_dropped_columns: true   flag_type_changes: true   flag_column_order_change: true    # WARN for CSV, FAIL for FWF dataset_name: "customer_accounts" folder: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/" format: csv                 # csv | fwf encoding: "UTF-8" delimiter: ","              # csv only fwf_widths: ~               # fwf only fwf_col_names: ~            # fwf only  # Optional: explicit file paths override folder-scan version detection # current_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260410.csv" # previous_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260401.csv"  # Optional: schema contract expected_columns:   - id   - name   - country_code   - account_status   - account_balance   - created_date  # Optional: key columns that must be unique key_columns:   - id  # Optional rule overrides (merged over global defaults) rule_overrides:   max_missing_rate: 0.02   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.05   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.005   min_row_count: 1000  # Optional per-column rules column_rules:   country_code:     allowed_values: ["GB", "US", "DE", "FR"]     pattern: "^[A-Z]{2}$"   account_status:     allowed_values: ["ACTIVE", "CLOSED", "SUSPENDED"]   account_balance:     min_value: 0     max_value: 1000000  # Optional custom checks custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Two modes: Folder scan (default): two recently modified files folder used. Newest = current, second-newest = previous. Explicit naming: current_file (optionally previous_file) set dataset config, paths used directly. Useful file naming conventions reflect delivery order, re-running check specific pair files. current file available (either via folder scan explicit config), run proceeds single-file mode: version comparison vs-previous schema checks skipped.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: File read entirely character columns via readr::read_delim() readr::read_fwf(). Leading trailing whitespace trimmed every value immediately reading. trimmed data frame used subsequent checks comparisons. raw file never modified.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Applied column trimmed data frame. Rules applied order: Known date formats tested: %Y-%m-%d, %d/%m/%Y, %m/%d/%Y, %Y%m%d, %d-%m-%Y. 90% threshold means column 10% bad (non-numeric) values still classified numeric, making bad values visible QC-11.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: check returns one dq_result objects: Single-snapshot checks (QC series) Schema contract checks (SC series) — expected_columns configured Version comparison checks (CP series) — skipped single-file mode CP-02 single source truth schema differences. new_cols_vs_previous missing_cols_vs_previous columns snapshots table derived directly CP-02 result — computed separately.
code: dq_result(check_id, check_name, column = NA, status, observed, threshold = NA, message) # status: "PASS" | "WARN" | "FAIL" | "INFO"

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: custom_checks_file dataset config points .R file. Sourced isolated environment (global env). Must define custom_checks(df) returning list dq_result objects. Receives trimmed data frame. Errors (missing file, missing function, runtime error) stop run. Results appear “Custom Checks” report section written column_snapshots (see C.8).

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Database tables created automatically first run via init_snapshot_db(). Table: snapshots Table: column_snapshots Rows written per column type: Custom check rows: one row per dq_result non-NA column value. dq_check = check_id, value = observed, threshold = threshold, severity_on_breach = status WARN/FAIL, else NULL.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Report filename: <dataset_name>_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>.html Console output:
code: run_dq_check(   dataset_name,        # matches <dataset_name>.yml in config_dir   config_dir = ".",    # contains dqcheckr.yml and <dataset_name>.yml   open_report = TRUE   # open in browser when interactive ) # Returns invisibly: list(status, report_path, snapshot_id) [dqcheckr] customer_accounts: FAIL - 0 warning(s), 2 failure(s). Report: reports/customer_accounts_20260410_143022.html

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: CRAN. pointblank dataCompareR evaluated used — logic implemented natively.

what: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
title: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
text: Automated data quality checks recurring dataset deliveries. new file arrival, dqcheckr runs single-snapshot quality checks, compares file previous delivery, writes self-contained HTML report, records summary statistics local SQLite database long-term trend tracking. Supports CSV fixed-width (FWF) formats. Custom organisation-specific checks can supplied plain R files.

title: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
text: Create two YAML files config/ directory: config/dqcheckr.yml — global defaults: config/my_dataset.yml — dataset-specific settings: run: HTML report opens automatically browser (interactive sessions). Historical run data accumulates SQLite snapshot database shown trend chart subsequent reports.
code: snapshot_db: output/snapshots.sqlite report_output_dir: output/reports/ default_rules:   max_missing_rate: 0.05   min_row_count: 0 folder: data/ format: csv library(dqcheckr)  result <- run_dq_check("my_dataset", config_dir = "config") result$status      # "PASS", "WARN", or "FAIL" result$report_path # path to the HTML report

title: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
text: Supply plain R file defines custom_checks(df) function returning list dq_result() objects: Point dataset YAML:
code: # config/my_checks.R custom_checks <- function(df) {   list(     dq_result("CC-01", "Revenue positive",               column  = "revenue",               status  = if (any(as.numeric(df$revenue) < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) "FAIL" else "PASS",               observed = "checked",               message  = "Revenue must be non-negative.")   ) } custom_checks_file: config/my_checks.R

title: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
text: package includes two runnable demonstrations using Star Wars dataset. Copy local directory run directly RStudio: open demo.R (named-file mode, CSV + FWF) demo2.R (folder-scan mode, version comparison, custom checks) RStudio, set working directory copied folder, source script.
code: # Copy to ~/dqcheckr_demo dest <- file.path(path.expand("~"), "dqcheckr_demo") file.copy(system.file("demonstrations", package = "dqcheckr"),           dirname(dest), recursive = TRUE) file.rename(file.path(dirname(dest), "demonstrations"), dest)

title: Construct a data quality result object — dq_result
text: check_id Character. Short identifier check (e.g. "QC-01"). check_name Character. Human-readable name check. column Character. Column check applies , NA_character_ row-level file-level checks. status Character. One "PASS", "WARN", "FAIL", "INFO". observed Character. observed (e.g. "5.2% missing"). threshold Character. configured threshold, NA_character_ applicable. message Character. Human-readable description result.

title: Construct a data quality result object — dq_result
text: named list seven elements: check_id, check_name,   column, status, observed, threshold,   message.

title: Construct a data quality result object — dq_result
code: dq_result("QC-01", "Missing rate", column = "age",           status = "PASS", observed = "0% missing",           message = "No missing values.") #> $check_id #> [1] "QC-01" #>  #> $check_name #> [1] "Missing rate" #>  #> $column #> [1] "age" #>  #> $status #> [1] "PASS" #>  #> $observed #> [1] "0% missing" #>  #> $threshold #> [1] NA #>  #> $message #> [1] "No missing values." #>

what: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
title: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
text: Automates quality verification recurring external dataset deliveries. new file arrival, runs single-snapshot quality checks (QC-01 QC-14, SC-01/SC-02), compares file previous delivery (CP-01 CP-08), writes self-contained HTML report, records summary statistics local SQLite database long-term trend tracking. Supports CSV fixed-width formats. Custom organisation-specific checks can supplied plain R files.

title: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
text: main entry point run_dq_check. Configuration driven two YAML files: global dqcheckr.yml per-dataset <dataset_name>.yml.

title: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
text: Maintainer: Mick Mioduszewski mick@mioduszewski.net

title: dqcheckr 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Single-snapshot quality checks: QC-01 QC-14 (missing rate, empty columns, duplicate rows, row/column counts, inferred types, numeric stats, distinct counts, allowed values, numeric bounds, non-numeric rate, key uniqueness, regex pattern, minimum row count) SC-01/SC-02 (schema contract). Version comparison checks: CP-01 CP-08 (row count change, schema diff, missing rate change, numeric mean shift, new/dropped distinct values, non-numeric rate change, column order). Custom organisation-specific checks via plain R file. Self-contained HTML report check tables, historical trend charts, column statistics appendix. SQLite snapshot database long-term trend tracking. Supports CSV fixed-width (FWF) file formats. Configuration via global dqcheckr.yml per-dataset YAML files.
